title: Wunder fleet
wikitext:
[]: Wunder fleet             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Aug 18, 2015 10:51 am Here are two questions. How many ships make up the Wunder fleet and how many kinds of ships are there?

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Aug 18, 2015 11:55 am SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Tue Aug 18, 2015 12:10 pm Yeah. I count at least fifteen aircraft carriers alone on that picture, though I might be overestimating. The sheer number of carriers in one fleet seems to suggest that WILLE are what's left of the UN military, as no other canonical force I know of has that many carriers at their disposal. Or maybe they just salvaged those ships after their original crews were tanged?

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Tue Aug 18, 2015 12:26 pm ^You know, now that I think about it, that does make quite a bit of sense. I think it's likely that the Wunder's fleet is what's left of existing fleets all around the world. Looks like the other ships are a smattering of Destroyers, Cruisers and what looks like at least 5 Battleships.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Aug 18, 2015 12:36 pm I updated my other post with two more images of Wunder's support fleet. It's weird. Wille comes off as this ragtag resistance group at first, but they have a huge amount of manpower and resources at their disposal. Look at this fleet of helicopters: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Aug 18, 2015 12:40 pm And yet they are mainly on the defensive - such is the power of the Nemesis series.

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Tue Aug 18, 2015 1:02 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: I updated my other post with two more images of Wunder's support fleet. It's weird. Wille comes off as this ragtag resistance group at first, but they have a huge amount of manpower and resources at their disposal. Look at this fleet of helicopters:

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Aug 18, 2015 2:17 pm I don't know how many threads this has been spread over before, but the wiki page is a nice start: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Warships#Warships_seen_in_Episode_08_and_Evangelion_3.0 http://forum.evageeks.org/post/738647/Real-world-military-vehicles/#738647

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Aug 18, 2015 2:29 pm Those are nice, but i want to know just how many of each ship there are. How many Kongo Destroyers? How many Ohio Submarines? How many Slava Cruisers? etc.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Aug 18, 2015 2:35 pm You could count them and tell us as easily as anyone else could; and if you have a particular interest in the matter that would be fitting.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Aug 18, 2015 8:38 pm The only battleships seem to be the Iowa class. I don't have a screen shot but 3.0 has a good like 5 second scene of one moving the 16in guns. But then on the map/3D display of the fleet I could have sworn there are 4 in the Wunder's fleet. I can't tell if it borrows from episode 8 and is a drawn version of the last 2(BB-65 and BB-66) which where never built because WWII ended, or if it is just 61-64. Btw off-topic a bit, but in Episode 8, it bugs the living shit out of me that they have BB-65 and BB-66 submerge when that is 1000% impossible.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Aug 18, 2015 8:48 pm There's a minimum of 5 Iowas in Wunder's fleet, so at least one extra one had to be built. Also why ever complain about cartoon physics?

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Aug 18, 2015 9:02 pm I must have missed the 5th one, I actually really like the idea that another one gets built in Rebuild-verse. But honestly I don't know how good they would do aganist targets like the nemesis series etc. We at least know that they could erase hills and do some serious damage to ground targets. But armoured Angel/Eva.... Topic is off... To answer your question, NGE held itself for the most part as being realistic. You could even argue that the Evas would be possible because they are Bio-Mechs, but then you have 2 Iowa class ships go under water and completely defy a lot of physicis. Just . But I guess you are right.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Aug 18, 2015 9:27 pm http://i4.minus.com/ibv8o4Tej34qJY.jpg http://i4.minus.com/ibtVBDrkH4Vvs7.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Aug 18, 2015 9:32 pm Sicarius VI wrote: Btw off-topic a bit, but in Episode 8, it bugs the living shit out of me that they have BB-65 and BB-66 submerge when that is 1000% impossible.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Aug 21, 2015 12:03 pm BlueBasilisk wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Aug 21, 2015 2:20 pm The Code 4A doesn't seem to be all that tough. They make short work of the one that attacks Unit 02 in the beginning. 4B has that powerful light weapon, but it fights Asuka under unusual conditions and it's implied Wille's never seen that configuration before. The Code 4C is no joke and it seems to be the one that gives Wille so much trouble. I get the impression Wille hasn't had the Wunder for very long, or at least not in its current state. It has a largely untrained civilian crew, the ship is still being equipped and weaponized when they launch, and it just seems odd that they'd wait so long to recover Unit 01 if it left their biggest asset at such a huge disadvantage. That just demonstrates how horrendously powerful Unit 01 is in its current state; twenty of these Eva-saucers and an N2 reactor couldn't even get the ship off the ground.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Aug 21, 2015 2:56 pm I'm hoping Over The Rainbow shows up

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Aug 21, 2015 3:10 pm Funny you should say that! Per TMB's earlier wiki link about battleships in Eva, it already has! The Over the Rainbow is really the USS Harry Truman under a new name, and it's listed as one of the ships in Wille's fleet. Wunder's escort has a few ships from the UN combined fleet.
